summary: Split the project-wide small-BSS assembly blob out of asm/sbss.s and redistributed .sbss declarations into the assembly translation units that own them. The Makefile stopped linking $(SBSS_O_FILES), while many asm files gained local .section .sbss blocks containing globals and .skip sizes. This was a repo-organization and linker-layout cleanup that made small-BSS data live with its corresponding module, improving locality for future decompilation and symbol migration.
what_changed: Removed the standalone sbss object from the main object list: Makefile changed O_FILES from including $(SDATA_O_FILES) $(SBSS_O_FILES) $(SDATA2_O_FILES) to only $(SDATA_O_FILES) $(SDATA2_O_FILES).
Deleted the contents of asm/sbss.s, reported as 3260 deleted lines, indicating the monolithic small-BSS assembly file was eliminated or emptied.
Added .section .sbss blocks to a large number of existing assembly files so uninitialized small-data symbols are declared beside their owning code/data module.

Preserved address-ordered labels and explicit allocation sizes through .global labels followed by .skip directives, such as lbl_804D7060 in asm/MSL/abort_exit.s and __AIS_Callback, __AID_Callback, __AI_init_flag, bound_32KHz, bound_48KHz, min_wait, max_wait, and buffer in asm/dolphin/ai/ai.s.

Moved named SDK globals into their module files where known, including __AR_Callback and related AR state in asm/dolphin/ar/ar.s, ARQ queues/callbacks in asm/dolphin/ar/arq.s, FstStart/FstStringStart/MaxEntryNum/currentDirectory/__DVDLongFileNameFlag in asm/dolphin/dvd/dvdfs.s, and BootInfo/BI2DebugFlag/AreWeInitialized/OSExceptionTable/__OSStartTime in asm/dolphin/os/OS.s.
Touched 138 files with 3791 added lines and 3268 deleted lines, mostly as mechanical placement of .sbss symbols into module-local assembly files.
smart_moves: Kept each .sbss block in the same assembly file as the corresponding text/data, which reduces the need to manage a central catch-all section during future decompilation.
Used explicit .section .sbss plus .global and .skip declarations, preserving symbol visibility and exact sizes without needing C declarations yet.
Updated the Makefile object list so the now-split sbss data is linked through the existing module objects rather than through a separate SBSS object class.

Retained known SDK-style symbol names where available, such as __ARQRequestQueueHi, __PADSpec, AlarmQueue, __OSArenaHi, HeapArray, NumHeaps, ArenaStart, and ArenaEnd, while leaving unknowns as address labels.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a monolithic data section, keep the linker ordering intact by moving symbols into the object that already occupies the matching place in the link order rather than changing symbol sizes or introducing new ordering assumptions.

For early-stage decompilation, .skip-based assembly declarations are a safe intermediate representation for untyped BSS/SBSS data: they preserve binary layout while allowing later replacement with typed C globals.
Small-data symbols accessed via r13/sda21 should remain in .sbss, not ordinary .bss, or codegen and relocation behavior may diverge.
Splitting data ownership across files makes future C conversion easier because each C file can absorb its own globals instead of coordinating against a central asm/sbss.s.
Known library and SDK globals should be named when evidence exists, but unknown entries can remain lbl_804Dxxxx labels to avoid inventing semantics.
naming_conventions: Unknown small-BSS symbols are kept as address-derived labels of the form lbl_804Dxxxx.
Known Dolphin SDK internals retain double-underscore names such as __AIS_Callback, __AID_Callback, __AR_Callback, __ARQ_init_flag, __PADSpec, __OSArenaHi, and __OSStartTime.

Some file-local or SDK globals use descriptive CamelCase or lower/camel names when known, including BootInfo, BI2DebugFlag, AreWeInitialized, OSExceptionTable, AlarmQueue, HeapArray, NumHeaps, ArenaStart, ArenaEnd, FstStart, FstStringStart, MaxEntryNum, currentDirectory, StopAtNextInt, LastResetEnd, ResetOccurred, Breaking, executing, and AutoFinishing.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Append module-local .section .sbss blocks after existing assembly content to house that module's small-BSS symbols.
Represent each uninitialized symbol as a .global label followed by an exact .skip byte count, commonly 0x4, 0x8, 0x20, or larger grouped gaps.
Use the existing object link order to carry SBSS placement instead of linking a dedicated asm/sbss.s object.
Avoid converting data to C in this PR; the change is a layout-preserving mechanical split rather than a semantic typing pass.
Preserve mixed known and unknown symbols in a single module section where the contiguous layout demands it, such as named AI globals interleaved with lbl_804Dxxxx padding/unknowns.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace module-local .section .sbss labels with typed C globals.
Check whether asm/sbss.s was removed entirely or left empty after this split.
Audit link-order-sensitive section splits involving .sdata, .sdata2, .bss, or .sbss after PR 188.
Search for remaining lbl_804Dxxxx symbols introduced by this split that later received semantic names.
